ncii-governance.name: NCII Entity Registration
ncii-governance.description: Ensure the organization is properly registered as an NCII entity with NACSA upon designation under Section 17. Maintain up-to-date registration details, designated contact persons, and respond to designation notices within the prescribed timeframe. Establish internal processes to track designation status and obligations arising from the Cyber Security Act 2024.
keyActivities: Respond to NCII designation notice within the statutory period specified under Section 17
Appoint and register a primary and alternate NCII liaison officer with NACSA
Maintain a register of all designated NCII systems and their classification status
Establish a process for handling changes to designation scope or entity restructuring

maturity.basic: Entity has acknowledged designation and registered basic contact information with NACSA. A single liaison officer is appointed but no formal backup or succession plan exists.
maturity.mature: Registration is actively maintained with primary and alternate liaison officers. Internal processes track designation changes, and registration updates are submitted proactively when entity structure changes.
maturity.advanced: Fully automated registration lifecycle management integrated with enterprise GRC platform. Proactive engagement with NACSA on designation scope reviews. Cross-functional governance committee oversees NCII obligations.

ncii-governance.name: NCII Governance Structure
ncii-governance.description: Establish a board-level governance structure with clear accountability for cyber security of NCII systems. Define roles and responsibilities including a Chief Information Security Officer or equivalent, a cyber security steering committee, and reporting lines to the board. Ensure compliance with NACSA directives on governance accountability under Section 18.
keyActivities: Establish a cyber security steering committee with board-level representation and defined terms of reference
Appoint a CISO or equivalent officer with direct reporting line to the board for NCII matters
Define and document roles and responsibilities for all personnel involved in NCII system security
Conduct quarterly board briefings on NCII cyber security posture, incidents, and compliance status

maturity.basic: A designated person is responsible for NCII cyber security but governance structures are informal. Board receives annual cyber security updates.
maturity.mature: Formal steering committee meets quarterly with documented terms of reference. CISO has dedicated resources and direct board access. Clear RACI matrix for NCII security responsibilities.
maturity.advanced: Integrated governance framework with real-time board dashboards on NCII risk posture. Independent cyber security advisory function. Governance effectiveness assessed annually with external benchmarking.

ncii-governance.name: NCII Compliance Programme
ncii-governance.description: Develop and maintain a comprehensive compliance programme to meet obligations under the Cyber Security Act 2024, NACSA directives, and sector-specific regulations. Include compliance monitoring, gap analysis, remediation tracking, and periodic self-assessments against the Act's requirements.
keyActivities: Maintain a compliance obligations register mapping each requirement of the Act and NACSA directives to internal controls
Conduct semi-annual self-assessments against the Cyber Security Act 2024 requirements
Track and remediate compliance gaps with defined timelines and accountable owners
Engage with sector leads and NACSA on interpretation and application of new directives

maturity.basic: Manual tracking of key compliance obligations using spreadsheets. Ad-hoc gap assessments performed when triggered by regulatory engagement.
maturity.mature: Structured compliance programme with dedicated resources, formal gap analysis methodology, and quarterly compliance reporting to steering committee.
maturity.advanced: Continuous compliance monitoring with automated control testing. Predictive analytics on compliance risk areas. Active participation in NACSA consultation processes for new directives.

ncii-governance.name: NCII Policy Framework
ncii-governance.description: Establish, maintain, and communicate a suite of cyber security policies specifically addressing NCII obligations. Policies must align with NACSA directives and cover all control domains. Include a policy lifecycle process with regular reviews, version control, and formal approval by senior management.
keyActivities: Develop and publish an NCII-specific cyber security policy approved by the board or senior management
Create domain-specific policies aligned with each NACSA directive and the 15 control domains
Implement a policy review cycle with annual reviews and event-triggered updates
Ensure all NCII system users acknowledge and attest to relevant policies

maturity.basic: Core cyber security policy exists but may not specifically address NCII obligations. Policy reviews are ad-hoc.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive policy suite mapped to NACSA directives with annual review cycles. Policy acknowledgement tracked for all personnel with NCII system access.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic policy framework with automated distribution, acknowledgement tracking, and policy effectiveness metrics. Policies continuously updated based on threat landscape and regulatory changes.

asset-management.name: NCII Asset Inventory
asset-management.description: Maintain a comprehensive, accurate, and current inventory of all assets comprising or supporting designated NCII systems. Include hardware, software, data repositories, network components, and supporting services. Assets must be classified according to their criticality to national critical information infrastructure operations.
keyActivities: Deploy automated asset discovery tools to maintain real-time inventory of all NCII system components
Classify each asset by criticality level, data sensitivity, and NCII designation relevance
Map asset dependencies and interdependencies across NCII system boundaries
Reconcile automated discovery results with manual registers quarterly

maturity.basic: Spreadsheet-based asset register covering major NCII system components. Manual updates performed periodically.
maturity.mature: Automated discovery integrated with CMDB. Assets classified and tagged with NCII designation metadata. Quarterly reconciliation and accuracy reviews.
maturity.advanced: Real-time asset intelligence platform with continuous discovery, automatic classification, dependency mapping, and integration with vulnerability and risk management systems.

asset-management.name: NCII Asset Classification & Labelling
asset-management.description: Implement a classification scheme for NCII assets and associated data that aligns with NACSA classification criteria and the Government's data classification framework. Apply consistent labelling to physical and digital assets, and enforce handling procedures commensurate with classification levels.
keyActivities: Define an asset classification scheme aligned with NACSA criteria and national data classification standards
Label all NCII system assets and data repositories with their classification level
Implement handling procedures for each classification level covering storage, transmission, and disposal
Review and update asset classifications when designation scope changes or system modifications occur

maturity.basic: Basic classification scheme exists with manual labelling of the most critical assets. Handling procedures are informal.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive classification taxonomy applied consistently across all NCII assets. Automated labelling for digital assets. Documented handling procedures enforced through technical controls.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic classification with data loss prevention integration. Automated reclassification based on content analysis and context. Classification effectiveness measured through compliance metrics.

asset-management.name: NCII Asset Lifecycle Management
asset-management.description: Govern the full lifecycle of NCII system assets from acquisition through disposal. Ensure secure procurement, configuration hardening before deployment, ongoing maintenance, and secure decommissioning with data sanitization. Track asset age and end-of-life status to prevent unsupported components in critical infrastructure.
keyActivities: Define secure procurement requirements for assets destined for NCII system use
Implement asset hardening standards applied before deployment into NCII environments
Track asset lifecycle status including warranty, support end dates, and end-of-life timelines
Execute secure asset disposal procedures with certified data sanitization and destruction records

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc lifecycle tracking. Disposal procedures exist but are not consistently applied. Limited visibility of end-of-life status.
maturity.mature: Formal lifecycle management integrated with CMDB and procurement. Automated end-of-life notifications. Certified disposal with auditable records.
maturity.advanced: Predictive lifecycle management with proactive refresh planning. Full chain-of-custody documentation from procurement to disposal. Automated compliance verification at each lifecycle stage.

risk-assessment.name: NCII Risk Assessment Programme
risk-assessment.description: Implement a structured, repeatable cyber security risk assessment programme for all NCII systems. Conduct assessments at least annually and upon significant changes to NCII systems, threat landscape, or designation scope. Apply a risk methodology acceptable to NACSA and produce risk treatment plans with defined remediation timelines.
keyActivities: Adopt a risk assessment methodology aligned with ISO 31000 and acceptable to NACSA sector leads
Conduct comprehensive risk assessments of all NCII systems at least annually
Produce risk treatment plans with prioritized remediation actions, timelines, and accountable owners
Report residual risk to the board and NACSA where risks exceed the defined risk appetite

maturity.basic: Annual risk assessments conducted using a qualitative methodology. Risk register maintained in spreadsheet format with basic risk treatment tracking.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual risk assessments using a hybrid qualitative-quantitative methodology. Integrated risk register with automated remediation tracking and board reporting.
maturity.advanced: Continuous risk assessment with real-time threat intelligence feeds, quantitative risk modelling (e.g., FAIR), and automated risk treatment orchestration. Risk appetite dynamically adjusted based on threat context.

risk-assessment.name: NCII Threat Modelling
risk-assessment.description: Conduct threat modelling exercises for NCII systems to identify potential threat actors, attack vectors, and attack scenarios relevant to national critical infrastructure. Incorporate intelligence from NACSA, NC4, and sector-specific threat advisories. Update threat models when systems change or new threats emerge.
keyActivities: Develop threat models for each designated NCII system using STRIDE, MITRE ATT&CK, or equivalent frameworks
Identify and profile threat actors relevant to the sector and NCII system type
Map attack vectors and scenarios to existing controls and identify coverage gaps
Integrate threat intelligence from NACSA advisories, NC4 feeds, and sector ISACs into threat models

maturity.basic: Basic threat identification performed during risk assessments. Informal consideration of sector-specific threats.
maturity.mature: Structured threat modelling using established frameworks for all NCII systems. Regular updates based on NACSA threat advisories and sector intelligence sharing.
maturity.advanced: Continuous threat modelling integrated with threat intelligence platforms. Automated mapping of emerging threats to NCII system attack surfaces. Red team exercises validate threat model assumptions.

risk-assessment.name: NCII Risk Treatment & Acceptance
risk-assessment.description: Implement a formal risk treatment process for identified NCII cyber security risks. Define criteria for risk acceptance, mitigation, transfer, and avoidance. Risk acceptance decisions for NCII systems must be approved by senior management and documented. Residual risks exceeding defined thresholds must be reported to NACSA.
keyActivities: Define risk treatment criteria and risk appetite thresholds approved by the board for NCII systems
Implement risk treatment plans with measurable milestones and completion tracking
Establish a formal risk acceptance process requiring senior management sign-off for residual risks
Report risks exceeding defined national threshold levels to NACSA as per directive requirements

maturity.basic: Risk treatment decisions are made by IT management. Risk acceptance is informal with limited documentation.
maturity.mature: Formal risk treatment framework with board-approved risk appetite. All risk acceptances documented with senior management approval and periodic revalidation.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic risk treatment optimization using cost-benefit analysis and quantitative modelling. Automated escalation of risks exceeding thresholds. Continuous monitoring of risk treatment effectiveness.

risk-assessment.name: NCII Business Impact Analysis
risk-assessment.description: Conduct business impact analyses for all NCII systems to determine the consequences of disruption, degradation, or compromise. Quantify impacts in terms of national security, public safety, economic stability, and essential service delivery. Use BIA outputs to inform risk treatment priorities and continuity planning.
keyActivities: Identify and quantify impact categories relevant to NCII designation including national security and public safety dimensions
Determine maximum tolerable downtime and recovery objectives for each NCII system
Map critical business processes to NCII system dependencies and single points of failure
Update BIA outputs annually and upon significant changes to NCII system architecture or designation scope

maturity.basic: BIA conducted for primary NCII systems with qualitative impact assessment. Recovery objectives defined at a high level.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive BIA covering all NCII systems with quantified impact metrics. Recovery objectives validated through testing. BIA outputs integrated with risk and continuity management.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic BIA with real-time dependency mapping and automated impact recalculation. Scenario-based impact modelling for multiple disruption types. BIA feeds directly into automated continuity orchestration.

access-control.name: NCII Identity & Access Management
access-control.description: Implement comprehensive identity and access management controls for all NCII systems. Maintain a centralized identity repository, enforce role-based access control, and implement the principle of least privilege. All access to NCII systems must be individually accountable and traceable.
keyActivities: Maintain a centralized identity directory for all users with access to NCII systems
Implement role-based access control with access profiles mapped to job functions and NCII system requirements
Enforce the principle of least privilege across all NCII system access grants
Conduct access reviews at least quarterly for NCII systems and revoke unnecessary access promptly

maturity.basic: Centralized directory exists for most NCII systems. Access provisioning follows basic request-and-approve workflow. Annual access reviews performed.
maturity.mature: Unified IAM platform covering all NCII systems with automated provisioning and deprovisioning. Quarterly access certification with manager attestation. Segregation of duties enforced.
maturity.advanced: Identity governance platform with AI-driven access recommendations, continuous access risk scoring, and automated remediation of excessive privileges. Just-in-time access provisioning for sensitive NCII resources.

access-control.name: NCII Privileged Access Management
access-control.description: Implement enhanced controls for privileged accounts on NCII systems including system administrators, database administrators, and network engineers. Enforce privileged access workstations, session recording, credential vaulting, and just-in-time access elevation to minimize the risk and impact of privileged credential compromise.
keyActivities: Deploy a privileged access management solution with credential vaulting and automated rotation for all NCII system privileged accounts
Implement just-in-time privileged access elevation with time-limited sessions and approval workflows
Record and audit all privileged access sessions on NCII systems with tamper-evident logging
Enforce use of privileged access workstations or secure admin environments for NCII system administration

maturity.basic: Privileged accounts are identified and documented. Password policies enforced but manual credential management. Basic logging of privileged actions.
maturity.mature: PAM solution deployed with credential vaulting and rotation. Session recording for critical NCII systems. Just-in-time access for most privileged operations.
maturity.advanced: Zero standing privileges model with all privileged access granted just-in-time. Behavioral analytics on privileged sessions with automated anomaly detection. Privileged access threat modelling informs PAM policy.

access-control.name: NCII Multi-Factor Authentication
access-control.description: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all access to NCII systems, with phishing-resistant MFA methods for privileged and remote access. MFA must be applied to all interactive logons, VPN connections, cloud management portals, and API access to critical NCII system components.
keyActivities: Deploy multi-factor authentication across all NCII system access points including local, remote, and cloud
Implement phishing-resistant MFA methods (FIDO2, hardware tokens) for privileged access and critical systems
Enforce MFA for all VPN, remote desktop, and cloud management access to NCII environments
Monitor MFA bypass attempts and failed authentication patterns as security indicators

maturity.basic: MFA enabled for remote access and privileged accounts on primary NCII systems. SMS or app-based OTP used as second factor.
maturity.mature: MFA enforced across all NCII system access points. Phishing-resistant methods deployed for privileged users. MFA events monitored in SIEM.
maturity.advanced: Adaptive MFA with risk-based authentication policies. Passwordless authentication for standard users. Continuous authentication using behavioral biometrics for high-security NCII system access.

access-control.name: NCII Remote Access Security
access-control.description: Secure all remote access to NCII systems through encrypted tunnels, endpoint compliance verification, and granular access policies. Implement network-level and application-level access controls for remote workers and third parties. Remote access to the most critical NCII systems should employ zero trust network access principles.
keyActivities: Implement encrypted VPN or ZTNA solutions for all remote access to NCII systems
Enforce endpoint compliance checks (patching, antimalware, encryption) before granting remote NCII access
Apply granular access policies restricting remote users to only the NCII resources required for their role
Log and monitor all remote access sessions with alerts for anomalous access patterns

maturity.basic: VPN-based remote access with MFA. Basic endpoint checks. Remote access logs collected but not actively monitored.
maturity.mature: ZTNA architecture for sensitive NCII systems. Comprehensive endpoint compliance enforcement. Real-time monitoring of remote access with alerting on anomalies.
maturity.advanced: Full zero trust architecture with continuous device and user trust evaluation. Micro-segmented access to individual NCII applications. AI-driven session risk scoring with automated session termination.

network-security.name: NCII Secure Network Architecture
network-security.description: Design and maintain a secure network architecture for NCII systems incorporating defense-in-depth principles. Implement network segmentation to isolate NCII systems from general enterprise networks, define security zones, and enforce traffic controls at zone boundaries. Architecture must be documented and reviewed annually.
keyActivities: Define network security zones separating NCII systems from corporate, public, and management networks
Implement micro-segmentation for the most critical NCII system components
Deploy next-generation firewalls at all security zone boundaries with application-aware rule sets
Maintain up-to-date network architecture diagrams and review security architecture annually

maturity.basic: NCII systems separated from general corporate network using VLANs and basic firewall rules. Network diagrams exist but may not be current.
maturity.mature: Defense-in-depth architecture with defined security zones. Next-generation firewalls with application-aware policies. Network architecture reviewed and updated annually.
maturity.advanced: Software-defined micro-segmentation with zero trust network principles. Automated network policy enforcement. Continuous architecture compliance verification against security design patterns.

network-security.name: NCII Network Monitoring & Detection
network-security.description: Implement continuous network monitoring for all NCII system environments to detect malicious activity, unauthorized access, and anomalous traffic patterns. Deploy intrusion detection and prevention systems, network traffic analysis, and integrate network telemetry with the security operations centre for correlation and response.
keyActivities: Deploy IDS/IPS at NCII network boundaries and critical internal segments with regularly updated signatures
Implement network traffic analysis with baseline profiling and anomaly detection for NCII segments
Collect and correlate network flow data, DNS logs, and proxy logs in the SIEM for NCII environments
Establish network monitoring dashboards with alerts for indicators of compromise and policy violations

maturity.basic: IDS/IPS deployed at perimeter. Basic network flow collection. Manual review of alerts during business hours.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive network monitoring across all NCII segments with IDS/IPS, NDR, and flow analysis. 24/7 SOC monitoring with SIEM correlation. Threat intelligence-driven detection rules.
maturity.advanced: AI-powered network detection and response with automated threat hunting. Full packet capture for critical segments. Automated containment actions triggered by high-confidence detections.

network-security.name: NCII DNS Security
network-security.description: Secure DNS infrastructure supporting NCII systems against manipulation, tunnelling, and abuse. Implement DNS filtering, DNSSEC validation, encrypted DNS protocols, and DNS query logging. DNS is a critical attack vector and requires dedicated security controls for national critical infrastructure environments.
keyActivities: Deploy protective DNS filtering to block access to known malicious domains from NCII networks
Implement DNSSEC validation on all DNS resolvers serving NCII systems
Enable comprehensive DNS query logging with integration to SIEM for anomaly detection
Monitor for DNS tunnelling and exfiltration attempts from NCII network segments

maturity.basic: DNS filtering service blocks known malicious domains. Basic DNS logging enabled. Standard DNS protocols in use.
maturity.mature: Protective DNS with threat intelligence integration. DNSSEC validation enforced. DNS-over-HTTPS/TLS for external queries. DNS analytics for anomaly detection.
maturity.advanced: Real-time DNS threat intelligence with automated response. Machine learning-based DNS anomaly detection. DNS security integrated with broader network security orchestration.

network-security.name: NCII Network Access Control
network-security.description: Implement network access control mechanisms to ensure only authorized and compliant devices can connect to NCII network segments. Enforce device authentication, posture assessment, and network quarantine for non-compliant endpoints. Prevent rogue device connections to critical infrastructure networks.
keyActivities: Deploy 802.1X or equivalent network access control on all NCII network switch ports and wireless access points
Enforce device posture checks including patch level, antimalware status, and encryption verification before network admission
Quarantine non-compliant devices and restrict them to remediation networks until compliance is achieved
Maintain an allowlist of authorized device types and MAC addresses for the most critical NCII network segments

maturity.basic: MAC-based port security on critical NCII network segments. Guest network isolation. Manual device registration.
maturity.mature: 802.1X network access control with certificate-based device authentication. Automated posture assessment and quarantine. Integration with asset inventory for device validation.
maturity.advanced: Continuous network access evaluation with real-time device trust scoring. Automated remediation workflows for non-compliant devices. IoT/OT device profiling and segmentation.

incident-response.name: NCII Incident Response Plan
incident-response.description: Develop, maintain, and regularly test an incident response plan specifically addressing cyber security incidents affecting NCII systems. The plan must comply with NACSA incident notification requirements under Section 26, define severity classification aligned with national incident categories, and establish coordination procedures with NC4 and sector CERTs.
keyActivities: Develop and maintain an NCII-specific incident response plan with defined roles, procedures, and escalation paths
Classify incident severity using a scheme aligned with NACSA national incident categories
Define procedures for mandatory incident notification to NACSA within prescribed timeframes under Section 25
Conduct tabletop and simulation exercises at least semi-annually to validate the incident response plan

maturity.basic: Documented incident response plan exists covering NCII systems. Notification procedures defined but exercises are infrequent. Single incident response contact for NACSA.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive IR plan with defined playbooks for common NCII incident scenarios. Semi-annual exercises including NACSA notification simulation. Lessons learned systematically captured and applied.
maturity.advanced: Continuously refined IR plan with automated playbook execution. Regular joint exercises with NACSA and sector peers. Real-time incident coordination platform integrated with NC4.

incident-response.name: NCII Incident Notification
incident-response.description: Implement procedures to comply with the mandatory cyber security incident notification requirements under Section 26 of the Cyber Security Act 2024. NCII entities must notify NACSA of prescribed incidents within the timeframes specified in the Act. Notifications must include required information and follow the format prescribed by the Chief Executive of NACSA.
keyActivities: Implement incident classification criteria that identify which incidents trigger mandatory NACSA notification under Section 26
Establish a notification workflow ensuring initial notification to NACSA within the prescribed 6-hour window for significant incidents
Prepare notification templates containing all information fields required by NACSA prescribed formats
Submit follow-up and final incident reports to NACSA within required timeframes with root cause analysis

maturity.basic: Manual notification process with awareness of reporting obligations. Email-based notification to NACSA. Basic incident information captured in initial notifications.
maturity.mature: Structured notification workflow integrated with incident management system. Automated severity classification triggers notification procedures. Pre-populated templates ensure information completeness.
maturity.advanced: Automated incident notification via NACSA API integration. Real-time incident information sharing with NC4. Automated initial classification with human review before notification submission.

incident-response.name: NCII Security Operations Centre
incident-response.description: Establish or engage a Security Operations Centre with adequate capabilities to monitor, detect, and respond to threats against NCII systems. The SOC must provide continuous monitoring coverage, maintain detection rules aligned with threats to critical infrastructure, and have the capability to investigate and contain incidents affecting NCII systems.
keyActivities: Establish 24/7 security monitoring coverage for all NCII systems through internal SOC or managed security service
Develop and maintain detection rules and use cases specific to NCII system threats and attack patterns
Implement SIEM with log collection from all NCII system components with a minimum 12-month retention
Establish SOC performance metrics including mean time to detect and mean time to respond for NCII incidents

maturity.basic: Managed SOC service or internal team with business-hours monitoring. SIEM deployed with basic correlation rules. Limited NCII-specific detection content.
maturity.mature: Dedicated SOC with 24/7 coverage and NCII-specific monitoring. Comprehensive SIEM with advanced correlation, UEBA, and threat intelligence integration. Regular detection engineering and rule tuning.
maturity.advanced: Next-generation SOC with AI-augmented detection, automated investigation, and orchestrated response. Proactive threat hunting programme targeting NCII-relevant threat actors. Purple team exercises validate detection coverage.

incident-response.name: NCII Digital Forensics Capability
incident-response.description: Maintain or have access to digital forensics capabilities for investigating cyber security incidents affecting NCII systems. Ensure forensic readiness through proactive evidence preservation, chain-of-custody procedures, and coordination protocols with NACSA and law enforcement as required under the Cyber Security Act 2024.
keyActivities: Establish forensic readiness procedures including evidence preservation, chain-of-custody documentation, and forensic imaging protocols
Maintain or contract access to certified digital forensics investigators with experience in critical infrastructure incidents
Ensure NCII systems generate and retain forensically relevant logs and artefacts with integrity protection
Define coordination procedures with NACSA-authorized investigators and law enforcement under Section 27-28

maturity.basic: Basic forensic capability through external contractor arrangements. Ad-hoc evidence collection procedures. Awareness of legal requirements for evidence handling.
maturity.mature: Internal forensic readiness programme with trained staff and forensic toolkits. Documented chain-of-custody procedures. Pre-established relationships with NACSA-authorized forensic investigators.
maturity.advanced: Enterprise forensic capability with remote collection, cloud forensics, and memory forensics. Automated evidence collection and preservation triggered during incident response. Forensic-grade logging across all NCII systems.

bcdr.name: NCII Business Continuity Plan
bcdr.description: Develop and maintain business continuity plans for all NCII systems ensuring the continued availability of critical national infrastructure services during and after cyber security incidents or disruptions. Plans must define recovery time objectives and recovery point objectives aligned with national critical infrastructure availability requirements.
keyActivities: Develop business continuity plans for each designated NCII system based on BIA outputs
Define and validate recovery time objectives (RTO) and recovery point objectives (RPO) appropriate for NCII criticality
Establish manual fallback procedures for critical NCII services to maintain minimum service levels during outages
Test business continuity plans at least annually through structured exercises and update based on lessons learned

maturity.basic: Basic continuity plans exist for primary NCII systems. RTOs and RPOs defined at a high level. Annual desktop exercises conducted.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive continuity plans with validated RTOs and RPOs. Semi-annual testing including failover exercises. Plans updated based on exercise findings and system changes.
maturity.advanced: Automated failover and recovery orchestration for critical NCII systems. Continuous readiness validation. Chaos engineering practices applied to verify resilience assumptions.

bcdr.name: NCII Backup & Recovery
bcdr.description: Implement robust backup and recovery capabilities for all NCII systems and data. Backups must be protected against ransomware through immutability, air-gapping, or offline storage. Recovery procedures must be regularly tested to validate that RPOs and RTOs can be achieved.
keyActivities: Implement automated backup schedules for all NCII systems aligned with defined RPOs
Maintain at least one immutable or air-gapped backup copy protected from ransomware and insider threats
Test backup restoration quarterly for critical NCII systems and validate data integrity post-restoration
Encrypt backup data at rest and in transit with key management independent of primary NCII system credentials

maturity.basic: Regular backups performed for NCII systems. Offsite backup copies maintained. Annual restoration tests for selected systems.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive backup strategy with 3-2-1-1 rule (3 copies, 2 media, 1 offsite, 1 immutable). Quarterly restoration testing. Backup monitoring with alerting on failures.
maturity.advanced: Automated backup validation with continuous integrity checking. Instant recovery capabilities for critical NCII systems. Backup infrastructure isolated in separate security domain with independent authentication.

bcdr.name: NCII Disaster Recovery
bcdr.description: Establish disaster recovery capabilities for NCII systems to restore operations following catastrophic failures or large-scale cyber attacks. Include provisions for alternate processing sites, infrastructure rebuilding, and coordination with NACSA and sector partners during national-level cyber incidents.
keyActivities: Maintain an alternate processing site or cloud-based DR environment capable of running critical NCII workloads
Develop and document DR runbooks with step-by-step recovery procedures for each NCII system
Conduct annual DR exercises including full failover tests for the most critical NCII systems
Establish communication and coordination procedures for DR activation including NACSA notification

maturity.basic: DR site identified and basic replication configured for primary NCII systems. DR procedures documented but infrequently tested.
maturity.mature: Active-passive DR configuration with regular replication validation. Annual full failover exercises. DR procedures integrated with incident response and NACSA coordination.
maturity.advanced: Active-active or hot-standby DR with automated failover. Continuous DR readiness validation. Multi-region DR capability with geographic diversity for national-level resilience.

audit-compliance.name: NCII Internal Audit Programme
audit-compliance.description: Establish an internal audit programme to independently assess the effectiveness of cyber security controls protecting NCII systems. Internal audits must cover all control domains on a risk-based cycle and provide assurance to the board and NACSA on the entity's compliance posture.
keyActivities: Develop a risk-based internal audit plan covering all NCII control domains on a rolling cycle
Conduct internal audits using auditors with independence from the functions being assessed
Track audit findings to remediation with defined timelines and escalation for overdue items
Report audit results and remediation status to the steering committee and board quarterly

maturity.basic: Annual internal audit covering key NCII controls. Findings tracked in spreadsheets. Audit performed by general IT audit team.
maturity.mature: Risk-based audit programme with dedicated cyber security audit capability. Automated finding tracking with remediation workflows. Quarterly reporting to governance committees.
maturity.advanced: Continuous auditing using automated control testing and monitoring. Real-time assurance dashboards. Audit analytics applied to identify systemic control weaknesses across NCII environments.

audit-compliance.name: NCII External Audit & Assessment
audit-compliance.description: Facilitate external audits and assessments of NCII systems as required under Sections 30-32 of the Cyber Security Act 2024. NACSA may direct NCII entities to engage authorized auditors for compliance assessments. Entities must cooperate fully and provide access to systems, records, and personnel as required.
keyActivities: Maintain audit readiness with current documentation and evidence for all NCII controls
Facilitate NACSA-directed audits by authorized auditors under Section 30 with full cooperation
Address audit findings within NACSA-prescribed remediation timelines
Engage independent external auditors annually for comprehensive NCII cyber security assessments

maturity.basic: Reactive approach to external audits with manual evidence collection. Audit findings addressed but often past prescribed timelines.
maturity.mature: Audit-ready posture with organized evidence repositories. External assessments scheduled proactively. Findings addressed within prescribed timelines with formal remediation plans.
maturity.advanced: Continuous compliance evidence collection with automated control attestation. Pre-audit self-assessments identify and remediate gaps proactively. External audit results benchmarked against sector peers.

audit-compliance.name: NCII Continuous Compliance Monitoring
audit-compliance.description: Implement continuous compliance monitoring capabilities to track the NCII entity's adherence to the Cyber Security Act 2024, NACSA directives, and internal policies in near real-time. Automated compliance checks reduce reliance on periodic assessments and enable rapid identification and remediation of compliance drift.
keyActivities: Deploy automated compliance monitoring tools that continuously assess NCII system configurations against security baselines
Map automated checks to specific requirements of the Cyber Security Act 2024 and NACSA directives
Generate compliance dashboards with real-time status for each control domain and NACSA requirement
Alert on compliance deviations and integrate with remediation workflows for automated or guided resolution

maturity.basic: Periodic compliance scans using configuration assessment tools. Manual mapping of results to regulatory requirements.
maturity.mature: Automated compliance monitoring for technical controls with mapped regulatory requirements. Dashboard reporting with deviation alerts. Integration with ticketing for remediation.
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive continuous compliance platform covering technical and process controls. Predictive compliance analytics. Automated remediation for common deviations. Real-time regulatory reporting capability.

audit-compliance.name: NCII Compliance Evidence Management
audit-compliance.description: Establish systematic processes for collecting, organizing, and maintaining evidence of compliance with the Cyber Security Act 2024 and NACSA directives. Evidence must be sufficient to demonstrate control effectiveness to authorized auditors and NACSA during assessments under Sections 30-32.
keyActivities: Define evidence requirements for each NCII control mapped to Cyber Security Act sections and NACSA directives
Implement a centralized evidence repository with version control and access restrictions
Automate evidence collection where possible, capturing system-generated artefacts and screenshots
Maintain evidence retention schedules aligned with NACSA requirements and audit cycles

maturity.basic: Evidence collected ad-hoc during audit preparation. Stored in shared drives or email. Manual collection processes.
maturity.mature: Centralized evidence management system with defined collection schedules and responsibilities. Evidence mapped to controls with version history.
maturity.advanced: Automated evidence collection platform with continuous harvesting from systems. Evidence integrity verified through hashing. Machine-readable evidence enables automated audit support.

supply-chain.name: NCII Vendor Risk Assessment
supply-chain.description: Conduct cyber security risk assessments of all vendors, suppliers, and service providers with access to or impact on NCII systems. Assessments must evaluate the vendor's security posture, data handling practices, and potential to introduce risk to critical infrastructure. High-risk vendors require enhanced due diligence and ongoing monitoring.
keyActivities: Classify vendors by risk tier based on their level of access to or impact on NCII systems
Conduct security assessments of high and critical-risk vendors using standardized questionnaires and evidence review
Evaluate vendor security certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2) and their relevance to NCII service delivery
Reassess vendor risk annually and upon significant changes to the vendor relationship or service scope

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc vendor security assessments for major NCII system vendors. Basic security questionnaires. Limited ongoing monitoring.
maturity.mature: Tiered vendor risk assessment programme with standardized methodology. Evidence-based assessments for high-risk vendors. Annual reassessments with defined triggers for event-driven reviews.
maturity.advanced: Continuous vendor risk monitoring with automated security rating integration. Real-time threat intelligence on vendor ecosystem. Vendor risk quantification integrated with enterprise risk management.

supply-chain.name: NCII Supply Chain Security Contracts
supply-chain.description: Incorporate cyber security requirements into contracts with vendors and service providers involved in NCII systems. Contractual provisions must address security standards, incident notification obligations, audit rights, data protection, and compliance with the Cyber Security Act 2024 where applicable.
keyActivities: Include mandatory cyber security clauses in all contracts involving NCII system access or data
Require vendors to notify the NCII entity of security incidents that may affect NCII systems within defined timeframes
Include right-to-audit clauses allowing the entity and NACSA-authorized auditors to assess vendor security
Define data handling, retention, and return/destruction requirements for vendor-held NCII data

maturity.basic: Standard security clauses in new contracts. Existing contracts may lack comprehensive security provisions. Basic NDA requirements.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive security schedules in all NCII-related contracts. Incident notification, audit rights, and data handling requirements consistently applied. Contract compliance tracked.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic contract management with automated compliance tracking against security provisions. Vendor security SLAs with performance penalties. Contract terms updated proactively based on evolving threats and regulations.

supply-chain.name: NCII Supply Chain Monitoring
supply-chain.description: Implement ongoing monitoring of the cyber security posture and risk profile of vendors and service providers in the NCII supply chain. Monitor for vendor security incidents, financial instability, geopolitical risk, and changes in vendor security certifications that could impact NCII system security.
keyActivities: Subscribe to security rating services for continuous monitoring of critical NCII vendor security posture
Monitor threat intelligence feeds for breaches or vulnerabilities affecting NCII supply chain vendors
Track vendor security certification status and investigate lapses or scope changes
Conduct periodic vendor security reviews with on-site assessments for the highest-risk NCII vendors

maturity.basic: Reactive monitoring through vendor self-reporting and periodic questionnaire updates. Limited visibility of vendor security changes.
maturity.mature: Continuous security rating monitoring for critical vendors. Automated alerts on significant vendor security events. Periodic on-site reviews for highest-risk vendors.
maturity.advanced: Real-time supply chain risk intelligence with automated risk recalculation. Vendor security telemetry integration where feasible. Supply chain attack simulation exercises.

awareness-training.name: NCII Security Awareness Programme
awareness-training.description: Implement a comprehensive security awareness programme for all personnel with access to NCII systems. The programme must cover the Cyber Security Act 2024 obligations, threat landscape relevant to critical infrastructure, social engineering awareness, and individual responsibilities for protecting NCII systems and data.
keyActivities: Deliver mandatory security awareness training to all personnel with NCII system access upon joining and annually
Include NCII-specific content covering the Cyber Security Act 2024, incident reporting obligations, and data handling requirements
Conduct regular phishing simulation exercises targeting NCII system users with tracked results and remediation
Measure awareness programme effectiveness through assessment scores, phishing simulation results, and security incident metrics

maturity.basic: Annual security awareness training delivered via e-learning. Generic content with limited NCII-specific material. Ad-hoc phishing simulations.
maturity.mature: Role-based awareness programme with NCII-specific modules. Monthly phishing simulations with progressive difficulty. Training effectiveness measured and reported to governance.
maturity.advanced: Adaptive awareness platform with personalized learning paths based on individual risk profile and behavior. Continuous micro-learning integrated into daily workflows. Culture measurement and behavioral analytics.

awareness-training.name: NCII Technical Security Training
awareness-training.description: Provide specialized technical security training for personnel responsible for operating, maintaining, and securing NCII systems. Training must cover secure administration practices, incident detection and response, and the specific technologies and architectures deployed in NCII environments.
keyActivities: Identify role-specific technical training requirements for NCII system administrators, security analysts, and incident responders
Provide specialized training on NCII system technologies, security tools, and secure configuration standards
Ensure incident response team members maintain relevant certifications and participate in regular exercises
Track training completion and certification status with alerts for expiring qualifications

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc technical training based on individual requests. Some team members hold relevant certifications. Training budget allocated but not systematically managed.
maturity.mature: Structured technical training programme with defined requirements by role. Training tracked in HR system. Annual training plans aligned with skill gap assessments.
maturity.advanced: Continuous skills development with hands-on labs, cyber ranges, and capture-the-flag exercises. Training effectiveness validated through practical assessments. Career development pathways for NCII security specialists.

awareness-training.name: NCII Board Cyber Security Awareness
awareness-training.description: Ensure board members and senior management of NCII entities have adequate cyber security awareness to fulfil their governance responsibilities under the Cyber Security Act 2024. Board-level briefings must cover the entity's NCII risk posture, regulatory obligations, incident trends, and strategic cyber security investment requirements.
keyActivities: Deliver annual cyber security briefings to the board covering NCII risk posture and regulatory obligations
Provide board members with relevant threat intelligence briefings on threats to the entity's critical infrastructure sector
Brief the board on Cyber Security Act 2024 obligations, potential penalties, and compliance status
Facilitate board participation in cyber security tabletop exercises at least annually

maturity.basic: Annual board presentation on cyber security status. Board members have general awareness of NCII obligations.
maturity.mature: Quarterly board briefings with NCII risk dashboards. Board members participate in tabletop exercises. Board-level cyber security committee established.
maturity.advanced: Board has real-time access to cyber security posture dashboards. External expert briefings on sector threats. Board cyber security competency assessment and development programme.

vulnerability-management.name: NCII Vulnerability Scanning
vulnerability-management.description: Implement regular vulnerability scanning of all NCII systems to identify known security weaknesses. Scanning must cover network infrastructure, operating systems, applications, and databases. Critical and high-severity vulnerabilities on NCII systems must be remediated within timeframes prescribed by NACSA directives.
keyActivities: Conduct authenticated vulnerability scans of all NCII systems at least monthly
Scan for vulnerabilities across infrastructure, operating systems, applications, databases, and container images
Prioritize vulnerability remediation using risk-based scoring that considers NCII system criticality and threat context
Track vulnerability remediation against NACSA-prescribed timelines: critical within 48 hours, high within 14 days

maturity.basic: Quarterly vulnerability scans of NCII perimeter systems. Unauthenticated scans with manual remediation tracking.
maturity.mature: Monthly authenticated scans across all NCII assets. Risk-based prioritization with automated ticket creation. Remediation tracked against SLA targets.
maturity.advanced: Continuous vulnerability assessment with agent-based scanning. Vulnerability intelligence enrichment with exploit prediction and threat context. Automated patching for routine vulnerabilities.

vulnerability-management.name: NCII Patch Management
vulnerability-management.description: Implement a structured patch management process for all NCII systems ensuring timely application of security patches. Patch deployment must balance the urgency of vulnerability remediation against the stability requirements of critical infrastructure systems. Emergency patching procedures must exist for actively exploited vulnerabilities.
keyActivities: Maintain a patch management policy defining timelines for patch deployment based on severity and NCII system criticality
Test patches in a staging environment representative of NCII production systems before deployment
Implement automated patch deployment where feasible with rollback capability for NCII systems
Establish emergency patching procedures for zero-day and actively exploited vulnerabilities with expedited approval

maturity.basic: Monthly patch cycle for NCII systems with manual deployment. Limited staging environment. Ad-hoc emergency patching.
maturity.mature: Automated patch management with staging environment validation. Defined SLAs by severity. Emergency patching process with accelerated approval workflow.
maturity.advanced: Continuous patching with automated testing, canary deployments, and automated rollback. Virtual patching for systems requiring extended patch windows. Patch intelligence predicting patch stability.

vulnerability-management.name: NCII Penetration Testing
vulnerability-management.description: Conduct regular penetration testing of NCII systems to identify exploitable vulnerabilities and test the effectiveness of security controls. Penetration tests must be performed by qualified professionals and cover external, internal, application, and social engineering attack vectors relevant to critical infrastructure threats.
keyActivities: Conduct annual penetration tests of all NCII systems covering external perimeter, internal network, and application layers
Engage CREST, OSCP, or equivalent-certified penetration testers with critical infrastructure experience
Include scenario-based testing simulating threat actor techniques relevant to the NCII sector using MITRE ATT&CK
Remediate critical and high-severity findings within prescribed timelines and validate remediation through retesting

maturity.basic: Annual external penetration test of NCII perimeter. Findings remediated but retesting not consistently performed.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual penetration testing covering external, internal, and application layers. Scenario-based tests aligned with sector threat profile. Findings tracked with retesting validation.
maturity.advanced: Continuous penetration testing programme with red team exercises simulating advanced persistent threats. Purple team exercises optimize detection capabilities. Attack surface management integrated with pen test findings.

vulnerability-management.name: NCII Vulnerability Disclosure Coordination
vulnerability-management.description: Establish processes for coordinated vulnerability disclosure related to NCII systems. Define procedures for receiving vulnerability reports from external researchers, coordinating disclosure with NACSA and vendors, and managing the remediation of disclosed vulnerabilities within appropriate timelines.
keyActivities: Publish a vulnerability disclosure policy defining how external researchers can report vulnerabilities in NCII-related systems
Establish a coordination process with NACSA for vulnerabilities discovered in widely-used NCII system components
Define disclosure timelines and coordinate with vendors on patches before public disclosure
Track disclosed vulnerabilities through remediation and verify effectiveness of fixes

maturity.basic: Security contact information published. Ad-hoc handling of vulnerability reports. Coordination with NACSA on significant findings.
maturity.mature: Formal vulnerability disclosure policy with defined intake, triage, and coordination procedures. Bug bounty or VDP programme for public-facing NCII systems. Coordinated disclosure with NACSA.
maturity.advanced: Mature VDP integrated with vulnerability management platform. Automated triage and routing. Active researcher community engagement. Sector-level vulnerability coordination leadership.

cryptography.name: NCII Encryption Standards
cryptography.description: Define and enforce encryption standards for protecting data within NCII systems at rest, in transit, and in use. Encryption algorithms and key lengths must meet or exceed NACSA and national cryptographic standards. Legacy or deprecated cryptographic protocols must be identified and migrated to approved alternatives.
keyActivities: Define approved cryptographic algorithms and minimum key lengths for NCII data classification levels
Enforce TLS 1.2 or higher for all data in transit across NCII networks with forward secrecy cipher suites
Implement AES-256 or equivalent encryption for NCII data at rest on storage, databases, and backup media
Conduct cryptographic inventory and migration plan for legacy protocols (SSL, TLS 1.0/1.1, 3DES, RC4)

maturity.basic: Encryption enabled for sensitive data at rest and in transit. Some legacy protocols may remain in use. Encryption standards documented but enforcement is inconsistent.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive encryption standards enforced across all NCII systems. Legacy cryptography identified with migration plans. Automated scanning for non-compliant cryptographic configurations.
maturity.advanced: Cryptographic agility with ability to rapidly rotate algorithms. Post-quantum cryptography readiness assessment. Hardware security modules for high-assurance NCII applications. Automated cryptographic compliance verification.

cryptography.name: NCII Key Management
cryptography.description: Implement comprehensive cryptographic key management for all encryption used in NCII systems. Cover the full key lifecycle including generation, distribution, storage, rotation, revocation, and destruction. Keys must be protected commensurate with the sensitivity of the data they protect.
keyActivities: Implement key management procedures covering generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction of cryptographic keys
Deploy hardware security modules (HSMs) for key protection in the most critical NCII system applications
Enforce automated key rotation schedules aligned with cryptographic best practices and NACSA guidance
Maintain key escrow and recovery procedures to prevent data loss due to key unavailability

maturity.basic: Key management procedures documented. Keys stored in software-based key stores. Manual key rotation performed periodically.
maturity.mature: Centralized key management platform with automated rotation. HSMs deployed for critical applications. Key lifecycle tracking with audit trails.
maturity.advanced: Enterprise key management with full lifecycle automation. HSM-backed key storage for all NCII encryption. Crypto-agile architecture supporting rapid algorithm migration.

cryptography.name: NCII Certificate Lifecycle Management
cryptography.description: Manage the lifecycle of digital certificates used in NCII systems including SSL/TLS certificates, code signing certificates, and machine identity certificates. Prevent service outages and security incidents caused by certificate expiration, misconfiguration, or compromise. Maintain a complete certificate inventory with automated monitoring.
keyActivities: Maintain a comprehensive inventory of all digital certificates deployed across NCII systems
Implement automated certificate expiration monitoring with alerts at 90, 60, and 30 days before expiry
Define certificate issuance standards including approved certificate authorities and minimum key lengths
Establish certificate revocation procedures for compromised or superseded certificates

maturity.basic: Manual certificate tracking for major NCII systems. Some certificate expiration monitoring. Trusted CA list defined.
maturity.mature: Automated certificate lifecycle management covering discovery, monitoring, and renewal. Centralized certificate inventory. Certificate pinning for critical NCII connections.
maturity.advanced: Full certificate automation with auto-enrollment, auto-renewal, and auto-deployment. Short-lived certificates where feasible. Machine identity management integrated with zero trust architecture.

physical-security.name: NCII Facility Access Control
physical-security.description: Implement physical access controls for facilities housing NCII systems. Restrict access to authorized personnel through multi-layer access control, visitor management, and monitoring. Critical NCII infrastructure areas must have enhanced physical security measures commensurate with their designation level.
keyActivities: Implement multi-layer physical access control with increasing security for zones closer to critical NCII infrastructure
Deploy electronic access control systems with individual authentication for all NCII facility entry points
Establish visitor management procedures including escort requirements and access logging for NCII areas
Conduct regular physical access reviews and revoke access promptly upon personnel role changes or termination

maturity.basic: Card-based access control for NCII facility perimeter. Visitor sign-in registers. Basic CCTV at entry points.
maturity.mature: Multi-factor physical access control for critical NCII areas (card plus biometric). Electronic visitor management with pre-registration. Access logs reviewed monthly.
maturity.advanced: Integrated physical and logical access management. Real-time occupancy monitoring. AI-powered surveillance analytics. Automated correlation of physical and cyber access anomalies.

physical-security.name: NCII Environmental Protection
physical-security.description: Protect NCII infrastructure against environmental threats including power failures, fire, flooding, temperature extremes, and natural disasters. Implement redundant environmental controls and monitoring systems to maintain NCII system availability and prevent physical damage to critical infrastructure equipment.
keyActivities: Deploy uninterruptible power supplies and backup generators with sufficient capacity for NCII system graceful shutdown or sustained operation
Implement fire detection and suppression systems appropriate for data centre and equipment room environments
Maintain environmental monitoring (temperature, humidity, water detection) with alerting in all NCII infrastructure locations
Conduct annual testing of environmental protection systems including generator failover and fire suppression

maturity.basic: UPS and basic fire suppression in primary data centre. Environmental monitoring with manual checks. Backup power available but infrequently tested.
maturity.mature: Redundant power and cooling in NCII infrastructure locations. Environmental monitoring with automated alerting. Annual testing of all environmental systems. Documented procedures for environmental incidents.
maturity.advanced: Fully redundant N+1 or 2N environmental infrastructure. Predictive maintenance using IoT sensors. Integrated building management system with automated environmental response. Regular disaster scenario testing.

physical-security.name: NCII Physical Monitoring & Surveillance
physical-security.description: Implement surveillance and monitoring systems for facilities housing NCII infrastructure. Continuous monitoring deters unauthorized physical access and provides evidence for security investigations. Surveillance coverage must include all perimeter entry points, critical infrastructure areas, and server rooms.
keyActivities: Deploy CCTV surveillance covering all entry points, perimeters, and critical infrastructure areas housing NCII systems
Implement intrusion detection systems (motion sensors, door contacts) for after-hours monitoring of NCII areas
Retain surveillance recordings for a minimum period aligned with NACSA requirements and organizational policy
Integrate physical security monitoring with security operations for correlated physical-cyber threat detection

maturity.basic: CCTV at main entry points and server rooms. Recordings retained for 30 days. Security guard patrols during business hours.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive CCTV coverage with analytics (motion detection, tailgating). 90-day retention. Integrated alarm monitoring with 24/7 response capability.
maturity.advanced: AI-powered video analytics with real-time anomaly detection. Integration with cyber SOC for physical-logical correlation. Automated incident alerting and response procedures.

cloud-security.name: NCII Cloud Security Governance
cloud-security.description: Establish a governance framework for the use of cloud services hosting or processing NCII system workloads and data. Define approved cloud service providers, assess data sovereignty and residency requirements, and implement the shared responsibility model with clear accountability for security controls across cloud and on-premises boundaries.
keyActivities: Define a cloud security policy specifying requirements for NCII workload deployment including approved providers and service models
Assess data sovereignty and residency requirements to ensure NCII data remains within jurisdictional boundaries as required
Map and document shared responsibility model responsibilities for each cloud service used for NCII workloads
Implement cloud security posture management to continuously assess cloud environment configurations

maturity.basic: Cloud usage for NCII systems is ad-hoc with informal security assessments. Data residency considerations addressed per-project.
maturity.mature: Formal cloud security policy with approved providers and service models. Shared responsibility model documented. Cloud security posture management deployed.
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive cloud governance programme with automated policy enforcement. Multi-cloud security orchestration. Cloud-native security architecture patterns for NCII workloads.

cloud-security.name: NCII Cloud Workload Protection
cloud-security.description: Implement security controls to protect NCII workloads running in cloud environments. Cover compute instances, containers, serverless functions, and managed services. Apply security hardening, runtime protection, and continuous monitoring specific to cloud-native deployment models.
keyActivities: Deploy cloud workload protection platforms covering VMs, containers, and serverless functions hosting NCII workloads
Implement cloud-native security hardening based on CIS benchmarks for the relevant cloud platform
Enable cloud-native logging and monitoring services with integration to the central SIEM for NCII workloads
Conduct regular cloud penetration testing and configuration reviews for NCII environments

maturity.basic: Basic cloud security features enabled (security groups, encryption). Cloud provider native security tools in use. Manual configuration reviews.
maturity.mature: CWPP deployed across all NCII cloud workloads. CIS benchmark compliance enforced. Cloud audit logs integrated with SIEM. Regular cloud-specific penetration testing.
maturity.advanced: Runtime protection with behavioral analysis for cloud workloads. Infrastructure-as-code security scanning in CI/CD pipelines. Automated remediation of cloud misconfigurations.

cloud-security.name: NCII Cloud Identity & Access Governance
cloud-security.description: Implement cloud-specific identity and access management controls for NCII workloads. Address cloud IAM policy management, service account security, cross-account access governance, and entitlement management. Cloud IAM misconfigurations are a leading cause of cloud security incidents and require dedicated attention for critical infrastructure.
keyActivities: Implement cloud IAM policies enforcing least-privilege access for all NCII cloud resources and services
Secure service accounts and machine identities with credential rotation and usage monitoring
Deploy cloud infrastructure entitlement management (CIEM) to identify and remediate excessive permissions
Enforce MFA for all cloud console and API access to NCII environments with conditional access policies

maturity.basic: Cloud IAM policies applied at project/subscription level. MFA enabled for cloud console access. Service accounts created as needed with static credentials.
maturity.mature: CIEM solution monitoring cloud entitlements. Service account credentials rotated automatically. Cross-account access governed through centralized policies. Federated identity with on-premises directory.
maturity.advanced: Just-in-time cloud access with automated provisioning and deprovisioning. Machine learning-based entitlement optimization. Zero standing privileges in cloud environments for NCII workloads.

change-management.name: NCII Change Control Process
change-management.description: Implement a formal change control process for all modifications to NCII systems including infrastructure, applications, configurations, and data. Changes must be assessed for security impact, approved through defined authority levels, tested before deployment, and documented with rollback procedures.
keyActivities: Establish a Change Advisory Board or equivalent authority for approving changes to NCII systems
Require security impact assessments for all changes to NCII system components and configurations
Mandate testing in non-production environments before deploying changes to NCII production systems
Maintain change records with full audit trail including requestor, approver, implementation details, and rollback plans

maturity.basic: Change request process exists for NCII systems. Security impact considered informally. Basic change documentation maintained.
maturity.mature: Formal CAB process with security representation for NCII changes. Mandatory security impact assessment. Testing gates before production deployment. Automated change tracking.
maturity.advanced: Automated change risk assessment and approval workflows. CI/CD integration with security gates for NCII application changes. Change analytics identifying patterns and predicting change risk.

change-management.name: NCII Configuration Management
change-management.description: Maintain secure baseline configurations for all NCII system components and enforce configuration compliance through automated monitoring. Configuration baselines must align with vendor hardening guides, CIS benchmarks, or NACSA-prescribed standards. Unauthorized configuration changes must be detected and remediated.
keyActivities: Define and document secure configuration baselines for all NCII system component types
Apply CIS benchmarks or vendor hardening guides as the foundation for NCII configuration standards
Deploy configuration compliance monitoring tools to detect drift from approved baselines
Implement automated remediation for common configuration deviations with alerting for significant drift

maturity.basic: Configuration baselines defined for key NCII systems. Manual compliance checks performed periodically. Hardening applied during initial deployment.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive configuration baselines with automated compliance monitoring. Configuration drift detection with alerting. Infrastructure-as-code for repeatable NCII deployments.
maturity.advanced: Continuous configuration compliance with automated remediation. Immutable infrastructure patterns for NCII workloads. Configuration security integrated into CI/CD pipelines.

change-management.name: NCII Emergency Change Procedures
change-management.description: Define expedited change procedures for emergency situations requiring immediate modifications to NCII systems. Emergency changes may be needed during active incident response, critical vulnerability remediation, or imminent threat mitigation. Procedures must maintain accountability while enabling rapid response to protect critical infrastructure.
keyActivities: Define criteria for invoking emergency change procedures tied to incident severity and threat urgency
Establish delegated approval authority for emergency changes with post-implementation CAB review
Require documentation of emergency changes within 24 hours of implementation with full justification
Review all emergency changes in the next scheduled CAB meeting to validate appropriateness and completeness

maturity.basic: Emergency change process exists but is informally applied. Post-implementation documentation often incomplete.
maturity.mature: Defined emergency change criteria and approval authority. Automated emergency change ticket creation. Mandatory post-implementation review within 48 hours.
maturity.advanced: Pre-approved emergency change playbooks for common NCII threat scenarios. Automated documentation capture during emergency changes. Analytics on emergency change frequency and root causes driving process improvement.

change-management.name: NCII Secure Release Management
change-management.description: Implement secure release management practices for software deployments to NCII systems. Ensure that releases undergo security testing, code review, and integrity verification before deployment. Apply software supply chain security measures including dependency scanning and build pipeline hardening.
keyActivities: Integrate static and dynamic application security testing (SAST/DAST) into the release pipeline for NCII applications
Implement software composition analysis to identify vulnerable dependencies in NCII application releases
Verify software integrity through code signing and checksum validation before deployment to NCII systems
Maintain secure build pipelines with hardened build environments and access controls for release artefacts

maturity.basic: Manual security review for major releases. Basic dependency checking. Software deployed through controlled process.
maturity.mature: Automated SAST/DAST in CI/CD pipeline. SCA integrated with vulnerability management. Code signing enforced for NCII application deployments. Release approvals gated on security scan results.
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive software supply chain security with SBOM generation. Signed build attestations. Automated policy enforcement across the entire release pipeline. Runtime application self-protection for critical NCII applications.
